title: Tephroseris italica
taxonomy: Tephroseris italica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Tephroseris, which is a group of daisy-like plants. The species is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. Its classification is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the class Magnoliopsida. Within the family Asteraceae, it is part of the tribe Senecioneae.
uses: Tephroseris italica, also known as Italian mountain everlast, has been traditionally used in folk medicine. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems are used to prepare herbal remedies. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat various health issues, including colds, fever, and digestive problems. The plant is also used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. However, further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Tephroseris italica as a medicinal plant.

intro: Tephroseris italica, also known as Italian hawk's beard or Tephroseris, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, providing relief from pain and inflammation, as well as supporting immune function. Tephroseris italica contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
